chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P. NO. S-114 OF 2025 __________________________________________________________________ Date Order with signature of Judge HEARING OF CASE. For hearing of main case 19.03.2025. Mr. Zafar Ali Talpur, Advocate for Petitioners a/w Petitioners. Mr. Abrar Ali Khichi, Additional Prosecutor General, Sindh Mr. Safdar Ali Depar, AAG along with -x-x-x-x-x- Mr. Arun Parsad, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent Nos.11 to 18, which is taken on record. The Incharge SHO, P.S. Gazi Khuwawar, is in attendance files Statement which is taken on record. It appears that the husband of Petitioner No.1 is allegedly involved in FIR No. 03 of 2025, registered at P.S. Gazi Khuwawar, District Shahdadpur, under Sections 302, 337-H, and 34 PPC, filed by one of the private Respondents. The concerned police have failed to arrest Ahmed Khan [husband of Petitioner No.1]. Upon inquiry, Petitioner No.1 stated that her husband has been missing since the occurrence of the incident. Be that as it may, it is the responsibility of the police to trace the whereabouts of the husband of Petitioner No.1. The SHO has assured the Court that if 15 days' time is granted, he will trace Ahmed Khan and produce him before the concerned Court of Law. The Petitioners are directed to cooperate with the Investigation Officer. However, it is made clear that whenever the concerned police approaches the Petitioners, they shall cooperate. In case of non- cooperation, the police shall be competent to take action in accordance with the law but shall not, in any manner, humiliate the Petitioners. The SHO is granted 20 days’ time to trace Ahmed Khan, failing which the concerned SSP is directed to take action against him.

In case of non- cooperation, the police shall be competent to take action in accordance with the law but shall not, in any manner, humiliate the Petitioners. The SHO is granted 20 days’ time to trace Ahmed Khan, failing which the concerned SSP is directed to take action against him. The SHO shall also ensure that no party takes the law into their own hands. If anyone does so, appropriate action shall be taken against them. To come up on 21.04.2025. JUDGE Jamil
